stories.title: **Headline:** Oklahoma Sweeps A&M Games 2 & 3, Clinches 14th Consecutive SEC Title — Wells Ends Regular Season at 36 HRs

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: No. 1 Oklahoma swept A&M in Games 2 & 3 to clinch the SEC title for the 14th straight year. Kai Minor hit a walk-off triple (G2) and homered to open G3. Kendall Wells ends the regular season at 36 HRs — one from tying the all-time NCAA record. #Sooners

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Kai Minor was the hero this weekend in College Station. Triple. Homer. Two clutch moments that saved OU's season series. Now Kendall Wells gets the SEC Tournament to chase Lauren Espinoza's 31-year NCAA record of 37 HRs. History is close. #Sooners #SECSoftball

article.headline: Oklahoma Sweeps A&M, Clinches 14th Straight SEC Title — Wells Ends Regular Season at 36 HRs

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Oklahoma Sweeps A&M, Clinches SEC Title

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn
- **Search Term:** Kai Minor Oklahoma
- **Caption overlay:** "14 STRAIGHT SEC TITLES"
- **Mood:** Triumphant, celebration
- **Notes:** Action shot of Minor rounding bases or celebrating in the dugout

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Type:** imagn
- **Search Term:** Kendall Wells Oklahoma
- **Caption overlay:** "36 HRs. ONE FROM THE RECORD."
- **Mood:** Focused, anticipatory
- **Notes:** Wells at the plate or celebrating; clean stadium background

---

research.facts:
Oklahoma Sweeps A&M, Clinches SEC Title — Wells at 36 HRs
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- OU won Game 2, 4-3 over No. 12 Texas A&M at Davis Diamond, College Station
- Kai Minor's two-out triple in the sixth inning was the go-ahead hit in Game 2
- OU won Game 3, 6-4 over Texas A&M
- Kai Minor homered on the fourth pitch of Game 3
- Ella Parker also homered in Game 3
- Kasidi Pickering homered in Game 3
- Oklahoma clinched the SEC regular season title — 14th consecutive year with conference title
- Kendall Wells ended regular season with 36 home runs (no HR in A&M series)
- Lauren Espinoza all-time NCAA record: 37 HRs (Arizona, 1995)
- Wells holds NCAA freshman record, SEC single-season record, OU program record
**Source:** oudaily.com, saturdaydownsouth.com, espn.com (box score), soonersports.com

stories.title: **Headline:** Nebraska Sweeps Penn State Doubleheader, Wins First Outright Big Ten Title in Program History

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Nebraska swept Penn State 5-1 and 10-4 to win the Big Ten regular season title OUTRIGHT — the first time in program history. 42-6. 22-1 in conference. Jordy Frahm and the offense both showed up when it mattered. #Huskers #BigTen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Nebraska trailed Penn State 4-1 in Game 2. Then Kacie Hoffman, Jordy Frahm, and Hannah Coor all drove in runs in the 6th. 10-4 final. Big Ten title. Program history. No. 1 seed in the Big Ten Tournament next week. #Huskers

article.headline: Nebraska Makes History: First Outright Big Ten Title After Penn State Sweep

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Nebraska Wins Outright Big Ten Title

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn
- **Search Term:** Jordy Frahm Nebraska
- **Caption overlay:** "OUTRIGHT BIG TEN CHAMPS"
- **Mood:** Celebratory, historic
- **Notes:** Frahm pitching or celebrating on mound

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Type:** imagn
- **Search Term:** Nebraska Cornhuskers softball
- **Caption overlay:** "42-6. PROGRAM HISTORY."
- **Mood:** Team celebration, triumphant
- **Notes:** Team celebrating on field or dugout

---

research.facts:
Nebraska Wins Outright Big Ten Title — Penn State Sweep
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Nebraska swept Penn State doubleheader (Game 1: 5-1, Game 2: 10-4) on May 2
- Nebraska final record: 42-6 overall, 22-1 Big Ten
- Jordy Frahm: Game 1 — 7 IP, 7 strikeouts, earned win
- Jesse Farrell: Game 1 — 2-for-3, double, solo HR
- Game 2: Nebraska trailed 4-1 entering 6th
- Kacie Hoffman: 2 RBI in 6th-inning rally
- Frahm RBI single tied the game
- Hannah Coor: 2-RBI double gave Nebraska the lead
- First outright Big Ten regular season title in program history
- First regular season title since 2014
- No. 1 seed in Big Ten Tournament (May 6-9, College Park, MD)
**Source:** huskers.com, wowt.com, si.com/college/nebraska, on3.com, cornnation.com

stories.title: **Headline:** NiJaree Canady Throws First Career Perfect Game on Senior Day — Texas Tech First Team to 50 Wins

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: NiJaree Canady on Senior Day: 5 perfect innings, 9 strikeouts, 62 pitches, her FIRST career perfect game. Texas Tech beats Baylor 8-0 and becomes the first team in the country to reach 50 wins (50-4). What a send-off. #WreckEm

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Texas Tech enters the Big 12 Tournament (May 7-9, OKC) at 50-4 with NiJaree Canady — who just threw a perfect game — leading the staff. The Red Raiders are the No. 1 seed. And they look like the real deal. #WreckEm #Big12Softball

research.facts:
Canady Perfect Game — Texas Tech First to 50 Wins
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- NiJaree Canady pitched perfect game vs Baylor on Senior Day, May 2, 2026
- Score: Texas Tech 8, Baylor 0 (5 innings, run rule)
- Canady: 9 strikeouts, 62 pitches, first career perfect game
- Sixth perfect game in Texas Tech program history
- Texas Tech record: 50-4 (first team in nation to reach 50 wins in 2026)
- Canady's 21st win of senior season
- Canady crossed 1,000 career strikeouts earlier in 2026 season
- Texas Tech is No. 1 seed in Big 12 Tournament (May 7-9, Devon Park, OKC)
**Source:** ncaa.com, kcbd.com, roundtable.io

stories.title: **Headline:** SEC Tournament Bracket Set — Oklahoma No. 1 Seed, Alabama No. 2, Florida No. 3, Texas No. 4 All Earn Double Byes

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: SEC Tournament bracket is set. Seeds 1-4 (double byes): No. 1 Oklahoma, No. 2 Alabama, No. 3 Florida, No. 4 Texas. Tournament starts Tuesday in Lexington, KY. OU plays Thursday. This field is absolutely stacked. #SECSoftball

article.headline: SEC Tournament Bracket Set: Oklahoma No. 1, Alabama No. 2, Florida and Texas Round Out Double Byes

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
SEC Tournament Bracket

#### Image Concept 1 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** gemini
- **Mode:** base_only
- **Prompt:** Aerial view of John Cropp Stadium at dusk, field lit up, SEC logo on pitcher's mound, clean dramatic sky, tournament bracket graphic overlay space, cinematic softball atmosphere
- **Caption overlay:** "SEC TOURNAMENT — MAY 5 | LEXINGTON, KY"

#### Image Concept 2 — Article Hero (1200x630)
- **Type:** gemini
- **Mode:** complete
- **Prompt:** Vibrant tournament bracket graphic with SEC softball branding, showing four double-bye seeds (Oklahoma No. 1, Alabama No. 2, Florida No. 3, Texas No. 4), crisp white and SEC blue color scheme, stadium background

---

research.facts:
SEC Tournament Bracket Set
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- No. 1 seed: Oklahoma (20-4 SEC)
- No. 2 seed: Alabama (19-5 SEC)
- No. 3 seed: Florida (17-7 SEC)
- No. 4 seed: Texas (16-8 SEC)
- Seeds 1-4 earn double byes (play Thursday)
- Tournament location: John Cropp Stadium, Lexington, KY
- Tournament dates: May 5-9, 2026
- Seeds 5-8 play Tuesday and Wednesday
- Selection Show: May 10 at 7 PM ET on ESPN2
**Source:** ncaa.com, secsports.com, si.com, saturdaydownsouth.com

stories.title: **Headline:** No. 10 Florida State Clinches Share of 19th ACC Title, Positioning for National Seed

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: No. 10 Florida State (45-8, 20-3 ACC) clinched a share of its 19th ACC regular season title. The Seminoles are projecting to host the Tallahassee Regional. ACC Tournament starts May 6 in Charlottesville. #FSUSoftball #ACCSoftball

stories.title: **Headline:** Wells Needs 2 HRs to Break the All-Time NCAA Record — The Chase Continues in SEC Tournament

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text: Kendall Wells. 36 home runs. Regular season over. The all-time NCAA record (37, Lauren Espinoza, Arizona, 1995) is ONE home run away from being tied. She gets the SEC Tournament to write history. 🥎 #Sooners #SECSoftball
